welcome.title: Your sports science command center
welcome.body: SportMind AI brings testing, analytics, and intelligent guidance into one professional workspace for elite teams.

audience.title: Designed for performance professionals
audience.body: Head coaches, sport scientists, physios, and analysts use SportMind AI to make faster, evidence-based decisions.

features.title: Testing, insights, and daily workflows
features.body: Run lab protocols, track athlete readiness, consult the AI coach, and build reports — all from one unified platform.

start.title: Sign in and set up your squad
start.body: Create your account, add athletes, and start recording tests. Your workspace is ready in minutes.

aiCoach.title: AI Coach
aiCoach.subtitle: Sports science consultant
aiCoach.welcome: Ask anything about training, recovery, performance, nutrition, or team intelligence.
aiCoach.referencePlaceholder: Reference library integration coming soon — peer-reviewed sources will appear here.

prompts.readiness: Analyze this athlete's readiness for today's training session.
prompts.injuryRisk: Explain the current injury risk factors and mitigation strategies.
prompts.trainingLoad: Recommend an appropriate training load for this week.
prompts.recoverySession: Suggest a recovery session based on current fatigue and wellness data.
prompts.nutritionRec: Provide a nutrition recommendation aligned with training and body composition goals.
prompts.generateReport: Generate a concise performance report for the coaching staff.
prompts.compareAthletes: Compare readiness and load across key squad athletes.
prompts.weeklyFocus: What should be the weekly team focus based on squad intelligence?

rolePrompts.coach: Which athletes should I prioritize in today's session and why?
rolePrompts.researcher: Summarize the key performance indicators with scientific context.
rolePrompts.physiologist: Interpret fatigue, recovery, and load trends for training periodization.
rolePrompts.sportsMedicine: Assess injury risk and recommend preventive interventions.

demographic.contextSubtitle: Profile used when interpreting this result
demographic.adjustedReferenceNote: Reference values adjusted for selected athlete profile

testingCenter.lowerIsBetter: Lower values indicate better performance for this test.
testingCenter.higherIsBetter: Higher values indicate better performance for this test.

bridge.validation: Please check the result value and athlete profile, then try again.
bridge.saveFailed: We could not save this result right now. Please try again.
bridge.unsupportedTest: This test type is not yet supported for scientific recording.
bridge.readFailed: Some results could not be loaded. Showing available records.

library.title: Test Library
library.subtitle: {{count}} protocols in the Sports Science library

custom.title: Custom Test
custom.subtitle: Define a coach-specific protocol
custom.basicSubtitle: Name, units, and protocol — defaults to screening evidence tier
custom.advancedSubtitle: Category, evidence tier, and primary metric key
custom.researchSubtitle: Required for clinical or research evidence tiers
custom.name: Test name
custom.duplicateKey: A custom test with this name already exists
custom.researchMetadataRequired: Validity and reliability notes are required for clinical/research tiers
custom.validationFailed: Please check the custom test details and try again

flexibility.name: Flexibility
flexibility.description: Range of motion and mobility screening

sprint30.name: 30m Sprint
sprint30.description: Linear sprint time over 30 meters from a standing start.
sprint30.protocol: Two trials after standardized warm-up. Best time recorded with electronic timing.
sprint30.aiRec: Maintain neuromuscular sessions if repeat-sprint trend is positive; monitor load before speed blocks.

squat1rm.name: 1RM Squat
squat1rm.description: Estimated or tested one-repetition maximum back squat.
squat1rm.protocol: Progressive loading with 3–5 min rest. Technical failure ends attempt.
squat1rm.aiRec: Align strength volume with readiness KPI; deload if fatigue exceeds target range.

cooper.name: Cooper Test
cooper.protocol: Standardized track or treadmill. Distance recorded to nearest meter.
cooper.aiRec: Use result to calibrate endurance microcycles and recovery between high-volume weeks.

yoyo.protocol: Yo-Yo IR1 audio protocol with 20m shuttle runs.
yoyo.aiRec: Track trend across mesocycles; reduce HIIT density if fatigue KPI rises.

beep.description: Multi-stage shuttle run to estimate VO₂ max.
beep.protocol: Standard beep test audio with 20m shuttles.
beep.aiRec: Compare level against position norms before intensive tactical blocks.

illinois.name: Illinois Agility Test
illinois.description: Timed agility course with direction changes.
illinois.protocol: One practice + two timed trials. Best time recorded.
illinois.aiRec: Pair with COD drills if below team average; avoid high COD volume when injury risk elevated.

cmj.description: Vertical jump from countermovement without arm swing.
cmj.protocol: Three trials on force plate or jump mat. Best height used.
cmj.aiRec: Monitor CMJ trend as neuromuscular freshness marker before match weeks.

sitReach.name: Sit and Reach
sitReach.protocol: Three attempts; best reach distance recorded.
sitReach.aiRec: Add targeted mobility if below reference and injury risk module is flagged.

yBalance.name: Y Balance Test
yBalance.description: Composite reach score for dynamic balance.
yBalance.protocol: Three reaches per direction; composite % of leg length calculated.
yBalance.equipment: Y balance kit or tape markers
yBalance.aiRec: Address asymmetry >4% with unilateral stability work before return-to-play progression.

bodyFat.name: Body Fat %
bodyFat.description: Skinfold or bioimpedance body fat estimate.
bodyFat.protocol: Standardized hydration and time-of-day. ISAK or device protocol followed.
bodyFat.aiRec: Integrate with nutrition planning; avoid aggressive deficits during heavy load phases.

visualReaction.name: Visual Reaction Test
visualReaction.description: Simple reaction time to visual stimulus.
visualReaction.protocol: 10 trials after familiarization; median time recorded.
visualReaction.aiRec: Low readiness plus slow reaction may indicate CNS fatigue — consider recovery day.

cmjRsi.name: CMJ RSI
cmjRsi.description: Reactive strength index from countermovement jump.
cmjRsi.protocol: Calculate RSI (jump height / contact time) from force plate trials.
cmjRsi.aiRec: RSI drop >10% vs baseline suggests neuromuscular fatigue — reduce plyometric volume.

fms.name: FMS Screening
fms.protocol: Seven movement patterns scored 0–3; composite and asymmetries noted.
fms.aiRec: Scores ≤14 or painful movements trigger corrective exercise block before load progression.

custom.name: Custom User Test
custom.description: Coach-defined metric with custom units and targets.
custom.protocol: Document protocol steps before recording for reproducibility.
custom.aiRec: Custom tests contribute to compliance tracking; link results to training objectives.

cloud.title: Firebase Readiness
cloud.subtitle: Cloud foundation for SportMind AI. The app uses local mock data by default until Firebase is configured and cloud mode is enabled.
cloud.firebaseConfigured: All required Firebase keys are present.
cloud.firebaseNotConfigured: Firebase is not configured. Add keys from .env.example.
cloud.dataModeHint: Set EXPO_PUBLIC_USE_CLOUD_DATA=true and configure Firebase to enable cloud repositories in a future phase.
cloud.cloudFlagWithoutFirebase: Cloud flag is enabled but Firebase keys are missing — app falls back to mock data.
cloud.envHint: Copy frontend/.env.example to .env and fill in your Firebase project credentials. Never commit real secrets.

analytics.kpiSubtitle: Today's readiness, load, and risk indicators
analytics.recommendationsHint: Rule-based guidance — replaceable by AI Coach

decision.readyBody: Metrics support a full training session with standard load.
decision.reducedBody: Elevated load with moderate fatigue — reduce volume 20–30%.
decision.recoveryBody: Prioritize sleep, hydration, and low-intensity recovery protocols.
decision.medicalBody: Injury risk elevated — consult sports medicine before high-intensity work.

rec.flexibility: Schedule dedicated flexibility and mobility sessions 3× per week.
rec.fatigue: Reduce training load and prioritize recovery over the next 48 hours.
rec.recovery: Add structured recovery: sleep hygiene, nutrition, and active recovery.
rec.injury: Implement preventive exercises and monitor pain markers daily.
rec.strength: Add progressive resistance training targeting lower-body power.
rec.compliance: Increase testing frequency to improve monitoring compliance.

summaries.medicalClear: No active medical flags. Cleared for full training load.
summaries.medicalRest: Active rest protocol. Monitor load and sleep daily.
summaries.medicalInjured: Injury management active. Follow RTP protocol and physio plan.
summaries.recoveryBody: Sleep and HRV trending stable. Continue current recovery block.
summaries.nutritionBody: Placeholder — connect nutrition logs in a future phase.

sections.sleepHint: Duration and subjective quality from last night
sections.physiologyHint: Hydration, resting HR, and session RPE

fields.rpeHint: Rate of perceived exertion from recent training

validation.sleepDuration: Sleep duration must be between 0 and 14 hours
validation.pain: Pain level must be between 0 and 10
validation.hydration: Hydration must be between 0 and 6 liters
validation.heartRate: Heart rate must be between 35 and 120 bpm
validation.notesLength: Notes must be 500 characters or fewer

recovery.title: Recovery Center
recovery.subtitle: Daily recovery intelligence from check-ins
recovery.centerSubtitle: Monitor recovery score and actionable guidance
recovery.allGood: Recovery markers look stable — maintain current plan.
recovery.empty: Complete a daily check-in to generate recovery insights.

recommendations.reduceLoad: Fatigue and RPE are elevated. Lower volume or intensity for 24–48 hours.
recommendations.recoverySession: Add active recovery: pool walk, light bike, or breathing work.
recommendations.hydration: Fluid intake is below target. Aim for 2–3 L across the day.
recommendations.sleep: Prioritize 7–9 h sleep with consistent bedtime and reduced screen time.
recommendations.mobility: Add targeted mobility for sore regions and pain-aware ROM work.

nutrition.title: Nutrition Center
nutrition.centerSubtitle: Daily macros, hydration, body composition, and smart recommendations
nutrition.logSubtitle: Track meals, hydration, supplements, and notes
nutrition.empty: Log today's nutrition to unlock insights.
nutrition.allGood: Nutrition markers look on target — maintain current plan.

bodyComp.screenSubtitle: Log measurements and track trends over time

hydration.reminderLow: Hydration is low — drink 500 ml now and set hourly reminders.
hydration.reminderMid: Approaching hydration goal — finish remaining fluids before evening training.

recommendations.increaseProtein: Protein is below target for training load — add 20–30 g at the next meal.
recommendations.increaseCarbs: Carb intake is low relative to session load — prioritize complex carbs pre/post training.
recommendations.drinkWater: Fluid intake is below target. Spread intake across the day with electrolytes if sweating heavily.
recommendations.recoveryShake: Add a post-session recovery shake with protein + carbs within 30 minutes.
recommendations.reduceCalories: Calorie surplus detected for fat-loss goal — trim snacks or portion sizes.
recommendations.electrolytes: High sweat risk — add sodium/potassium with fluids around sessions.
recommendations.mealTiming: Align pre-workout carbs 2–3 h before and post-workout protein within 30 min.

wearables.title: Device Center

wearables.mockSyncHint: Connect a provider first, then run a mock sync batch.
wearables.connectFirst: Connect a provider to enable mock sync.
wearables.availableSubtitle: Tap connect to link a new data source

syncHistory.empty: No sync events yet — connect a device and run sync.

deviceHealth.title: Device Health
deviceHealth.missingData: Some expected metrics have not synced today

sportsMedicine.title: Sports Medicine
sportsMedicine.subtitle: Injury prevention and return-to-play intelligence
sportsMedicine.workspaceSubtitle: Injury risk, RTP, and medical context
sportsMedicine.regionRiskHint: Rule-based risk from check-ins, load, and history
sportsMedicine.medicalNotesBody: Placeholder — clinician notes and external EMR links in a future phase.
sportsMedicine.medicalClear: No active injuries — cleared for planned load.
sportsMedicine.addInjurySubtitle: Log injury details and RTP starting phase

alerts.highHamstring: Elevated hamstring load risk — monitor sprint volume.
alerts.highAnkle: Ankle stability risk elevated — review balance and landing.
alerts.reduceSprint: Lower maximal sprint volume this microcycle.
alerts.mobility: Schedule targeted mobility for high-risk regions.
alerts.medical: Pain or risk thresholds exceeded — seek clinical review.

prevention.sleep: Protect sleep quality before high-intensity days.

teamIntelligence.title: Team Intelligence
teamIntelligence.empty: Add athletes to the roster to enable squad analytics.
teamIntelligence.teamDetailHint: Squad health, rankings, and alerts for this team
teamIntelligence.squadHealthHint: Aggregated performance, readiness, compliance, and risk
teamIntelligence.noRisk: No players currently flagged at elevated risk.

alerts.highInjuryBody: Injury risk exceeds safe threshold — review load and RTP status.
alerts.lowRecoveryBody: Recovery score is low — prioritize sleep, nutrition, and active recovery.
alerts.highFatigueBody: Fatigue is elevated — consider reducing intensity or volume.
alerts.lowComplianceBody: Training plan adherence is below target — review barriers with staff.
alerts.poorNutritionBody: Nutrition targets are being missed — align meal plans with training load.
alerts.overloadBody: Combined load, fatigue, and compliance signals suggest overload risk.

staff.rtpBody: One or more injured players need coordinated RTP planning with medical staff.
staff.nutritionBody: Team nutrition compliance is below target — schedule group education and meal monitoring.
staff.complianceBody: Several players are missing planned sessions — investigate scheduling and motivation.
staff.fatigueBody: Fatigue levels are elevated — rotate starters and add recovery sessions.
staff.positionBody: One position group is underperforming — assign position-specific microcycles.
staff.loadBody: Overload alerts detected — taper high-intensity work this microcycle.

trainingBuilder.title: Smart Training Builder
trainingBuilder.subtitle: AI-assisted individualized programs from athlete analytics
trainingBuilder.workspaceSubtitle: Today's plan, weekly load, and progress
trainingBuilder.noPlan: No active training program. Generate a personalized weekly plan from current analytics.

acwrZone.low: Under-training zone — consider gradual volume increase

trainingBuilder.weeklyPlanHint: Mon–Sun sessions adjusted for readiness and injury risk
trainingBuilder.allGood: Training load and readiness are balanced — proceed as planned.

recommendations.reduceIntensity: ACWR or fatigue elevated — cap RPE and reduce sprint volume.
recommendations.increaseVolume: Readiness is high and load is low — safe to add one quality session.
recommendations.recoverySession: Swap high-intensity work for active recovery today.
recommendations.mobilitySession: Include targeted mobility for high-risk or stiff regions.
recommendations.injuryPrevention: Add prehab work for hamstring, ankle, or groin stability.
